offline_notice: This file is a complete metadata snapshot. It has no runtime dependency on external catalogs or URLs.
normalized_records_notice: The records field is a convenient V1 index. source_documents retains the complete, authoritative source text, including nested translation and acquisition details.

records.title: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man
records.author: James Joyce
records.description: A young man's artistic awakening amid religion and nationalism.
records.why_included: Challenged for sexuality, profanity, religious criticism, and perceived obscenity.

records.title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
records.author: Mark Twain
records.description: A Mississippi River journey in which Huck travels with Jim, an enslaved man seeking freedom.
records.why_included: Frequently challenged for racial slurs, depictions of slavery, stereotypes, and disputes about how race is taught.

records.title: Areopagitica
records.author: John Milton
records.description: A foundational argument against state licensing and prepublication censorship.
records.why_included: Directly opposes systems that require official permission before publication.

records.title: As I Lay Dying
records.author: William Faulkner
records.description: A poor Southern family transports its mother's body for burial.
records.why_included: Challenged for language, sexuality, abortion, death, religion, and family dysfunction.

records.title: Babbitt
records.author: Sinclair Lewis
records.description: A satirical portrait of business culture, conformity, and middle-class aspiration.
records.why_included: Challenged for sexuality, alcohol, language, and criticism of civic and commercial values.

records.title: Camping and Woodcraft
records.author: Horace Kephart
records.description: Campcraft, shelters, tools, fire, and wilderness living.
records.why_included: An enduring practical reference, with medical and conservation advice clearly marked as historical.

records.title: Civil Disobedience
records.author: Henry David Thoreau
records.description: An argument against cooperation with profoundly unjust government action.
records.why_included: Defends individual conscience against coercive state power.

records.title: Common Sense
records.author: Thomas Paine
records.description: A direct argument that people may reject illegitimate political authority.
records.why_included: Challenges hereditary rule and locates political authority in the people.

records.title: Don Quixote
records.original_title: El ingenioso hidalgo don Quijote de la Mancha
records.author: Miguel de Cervantes
records.description: An aging reader of chivalric romances remakes himself as a knight and travels with Sancho Panza.
records.why_included: A foundational modern novel whose satire of inherited narratives, institutions, and certainty has made it enduringly controversial.

records.title: Dubliners
records.author: James Joyce
records.description: Stories portraying paralysis, disappointment, and daily life in Dublin.
records.why_included: Publication was obstructed over political references, sexuality, religion, and allegedly defamatory realism.

records.title: Surveillance Self-Defense
records.description: Current guidance on threat modeling, private communications, metadata, and device security.
records.snapshot_policy: Store review date, source URL, attribution, license text, and an unchanged snapshot where permitted.

records.title: Elementary Cryptanalysis
records.author: Helen Fouché Gaines
records.description: A substantial reference on solving classical ciphers.
records.why_included: Important historical cryptanalysis reference, but not yet U.S. public domain.

records.title: Elmer Gantry
records.author: Sinclair Lewis
records.description: A charismatic opportunist builds a career through organized religion.
records.why_included: Banned for profanity, sexual behavior, and its attack on religious hypocrisy.

records.title: Finding Your Way with Map and Compass
records.description: Map scales, contours, bearings, and compass use.
records.why_included: Civilian navigation reference suitable for offline use.

records.title: Survival
records.recommended_edition: Most recent unrestricted edition; retain 1957 edition as historical context.
records.civilian_relevance: Shelter, water, fire, signaling, navigation, weather, and survival psychology.
records.safety_note: Historical medical, food, and safety recommendations must be compared with current civilian guidance.

records.title: Map Reading and Land Navigation
records.civilian_relevance: Maps, compass bearings, coordinates, terrain association, and route planning.

records.title: Rigging Techniques, Procedures and Applications
records.civilian_relevance: Rope, knots, slings, hoists, anchors, and mechanical advantage.

records.title: Food and Water in an Emergency
records.description: Household food storage, emergency water, and power-loss preparation.
records.why_included: A concise federal reference for common disruptions.

records.title: Frankenstein
records.author: Mary Shelley
records.description: A scientist creates life and abandons the being whose suffering he caused.
records.why_included: Challenged for horror, violence, creation of life, suicide, religion, and perceived indecency.

records.title: Jane Eyre
records.author: Charlotte Brontë
records.description: An orphaned governess seeks autonomy, love, moral equality, and economic independence.
records.why_included: Attacked for female independence, sexuality, anger, and criticism of class and patriarchal authority.

records.title: Lady Chatterley's Lover
records.author: D. H. Lawrence
records.description: A novel about class, marriage, intimacy, and an affair across social boundaries.
records.why_included: Banned and prosecuted in several countries for explicit sexual language and adultery.

records.title: Leaves of Grass
records.author: Walt Whitman
records.description: Poetry celebrating the body, democracy, nature, and identity.
records.why_included: Suppressed for sensuality, same-sex themes, bodily imagery, and alleged obscenity.

records.title: Main Street
records.author: Sinclair Lewis
records.description: An idealistic woman confronts conformity in a small Midwestern town.
records.why_included: Locally banned or denounced for its unflattering portrayal of small-town America.

records.title: Make Water Safe During an Emergency
records.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
records.description: Current guidance on boiling, disinfecting, and filtering emergency water.
records.why_included: High-value life-safety guidance that must remain current and dated.

records.title: Manual for the Solution of Military Ciphers
records.author: Parker Hitt
records.description: A systematic historical guide to analyzing substitution and transposition ciphers.
records.why_included: Preserves a practical foundation in private communication and cryptanalysis.

records.title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
records.author: George Orwell
records.description: A novel of surveillance, controlled language, manufactured reality, and political terror.
records.why_included: Essential fiction for understanding authoritarian information control.
records.link_policy: Prefer rights-holder, publisher, author estate, or public-library locator.

records.title: On Liberty
records.author: John Stuart Mill
records.description: A defense of freedom of thought, expression, individuality, and limits on social coercion.
records.why_included: Directly challenges political and social suppression of dissent.

records.title: Propaganda
records.author: Edward Bernays
records.description: An early account of organized opinion management, symbols, authority, and mass persuasion.
records.why_included: Helps readers identify techniques that governments and institutions use to shape public belief.

records.title: Public Opinion
records.author: Walter Lippmann
records.description: An examination of stereotypes, media, expertise, and the construction of public belief.
records.why_included: Gives readers tools for recognizing manufactured pictures of reality.

records.title: Rights of Man
records.author: Thomas Paine
records.description: A defense of popular sovereignty and a critique of hereditary government.
records.why_included: Provides a compact case for political rights independent of dynastic authority.

records.title: Shelters, Shacks and Shanties
records.author: D. C. Beard
records.description: Improvised shelters and simple structures.
records.why_included: Useful historic construction ideas; structural safety must be independently assessed.

records.title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
records.author: Mark Twain
records.description: A mischievous boy navigates childhood, danger, conscience, and small-town society.
records.why_included: Challenged for racial language, disobedience, violence, and classroom suitability.

records.title: The Awakening
records.author: Kate Chopin
records.description: A woman questions marriage, motherhood, convention, and personal autonomy.
records.why_included: Condemned and restricted for female sexuality, adultery, and rejection of prescribed gender roles.

records.title: The Call of the Wild
records.author: Jack London
records.description: A domesticated dog is forced into brutal life during the Klondike Gold Rush.
records.why_included: Banned or burned for violence, evolutionary themes, and the author's political associations.

records.title: The Great Gatsby
records.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
records.description: A story of wealth, class, obsession, and self-invention in Jazz Age America.
records.why_included: Challenged for language, sexuality, adultery, alcohol, and perceived moral content.

records.title: The Jungle
records.author: Upton Sinclair
records.description: An exposé-driven novel about immigrant labor and the meatpacking industry.
records.why_included: Suppressed or attacked for socialist politics, labor criticism, and graphic industrial conditions.

records.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
records.author: Oscar Wilde
records.description: A man remains outwardly young while a portrait records his corruption.
records.why_included: Censored for homoerotic implications, decadence, drug use, and alleged immorality.

records.title: The Rape of the Mind
records.author: Joost Meerloo
records.description: A study of coercive persuasion and totalitarian psychological control.
records.why_included: A foundational reference for recognizing coercive ideological environments.
records.link_policy: Prefer rights-holder, publisher, author estate, or public-library locator.

records.title: The Republic
records.author: Plato
records.description: A dialogue about justice, government, education, censorship, art, and the ideal state.
records.why_included: Often restricted in ideological settings; it is also a crucial source on the logic of censorship itself.

records.title: The Scarlet Letter
records.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
records.description: A woman condemned for adultery lives under the judgment of a Puritan community.
records.why_included: Challenged for adultery, sexuality, religious criticism, and perceived immorality.

records.title: The Social Contract
records.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
records.description: An argument that legitimate political authority arises from collective popular sovereignty.
records.why_included: Banned and burned for republicanism and its challenge to established political authority.

records.title: The Sun Also Rises
records.author: Ernest Hemingway
records.description: Expatriates travel through France and Spain while confronting war trauma and disillusionment.
records.why_included: Challenged for sexuality, alcohol, language, and unconventional relationships.

records.title: The Trial
records.author: Franz Kafka
records.description: A man is prosecuted by an inaccessible bureaucracy without being told his offense.
records.why_included: A defining novel of arbitrary authority and institutional terror, suppressed under authoritarian regimes.

records.title: Two Treatises of Government
records.author: John Locke
records.description: A defense of natural rights, consent of the governed, and limits on political authority.
records.why_included: Supplies an enduring argument for removing governments that violate public trust.

records.title: Ulysses
records.author: James Joyce
records.description: An experimental novel tracing ordinary life in Dublin over a single day.
records.why_included: Seized and suppressed as obscene before a landmark United States court decision permitted publication.

records.title: Uncle Tom's Cabin
records.author: Harriet Beecher Stowe
records.description: An antislavery novel depicting families separated and abused under slavery.
records.why_included: Suppressed in the antebellum South for abolitionist content and later challenged over racial representation.

records.title: Winesburg, Ohio
records.author: Sherwood Anderson
records.description: Interconnected stories about isolation and frustrated lives in a small town.
records.why_included: Removed from libraries for sexual themes and alleged obscenity.

records.title: Wuthering Heights
records.author: Emily Brontë
records.description: Obsession and revenge shape two generations on the Yorkshire moors.
records.why_included: Condemned and challenged for cruelty, abuse, violence, class conflict, and religious irreverence.

sources.name: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.use_for: Current emergency water, sanitation, food-safety, and health guidance.

sources.name: Electronic Frontier Foundation
sources.acquisition_note: Confirm the license on the exact work or module before download.

sources.name: Library of Congress
sources.use_for: Historical scans, U.S. Army field-manual inventory, and bibliographic verification.

sources.name: Project Gutenberg
sources.use_for: Public-domain EPUB, HTML, and plain-text editions.
sources.acquisition_note: Record the exact ebook URL and retain no Project Gutenberg branding unless its terms permit it.

sources.name: Ready.gov
sources.use_for: FEMA preparedness guides and planning material.

sources.name: Standard Ebooks
sources.acquisition_note: Verify the edition page and any project-specific redistribution terms.

sources.name: U.S. Geological Survey
sources.use_for: Map, compass, topographic-map, and geographic-reference material.

source_documents.external-reading.yaml:
- id: nineteen-eighty-four

  collections: [essential-reading]

  description: A novel of surveillance, controlled language, manufactured reality, and political terror.
  why_included: Essential fiction for understanding authoritarian information control.

- id: the-rape-of-the-mind

  collections: [essential-reading]
  rights_status: copyrighted-link-only
  description: A study of coercive persuasion and totalitarian psychological control.
  why_included: A foundational reference for recognizing coercive ideological environments.
  link_policy: Prefer rights-holder, publisher, author estate, or public-library locator.
  tags: [propaganda, coercive-persuasion, psychology, nonfiction]
source_documents.manuals.yaml:
- id: fm-21-76-survival
  title: Survival
  publication_number: FM 21-76
  publisher: United States Army
  recommended_edition: Most recent unrestricted edition; retain 1957 edition as historical context.
  collections: [preparedness]
  distribution_status: verify-edition
  civilian_relevance: Shelter, water, fire, signaling, navigation, weather, and survival psychology.
  safety_note: Historical medical, food, and safety recommendations must be compared with current civilian guidance.
  tags: [survival, navigation, water, fieldcraft]

- id: fm-3-25-26-map-reading-and-land-navigation
  title: Map Reading and Land Navigation
  publication_number: FM 3-25.26
  publisher: United States Army
  collections: [preparedness]
  distribution_status: verify-edition
  civilian_relevance: Maps, compass bearings, coordinates, terrain association, and route planning.
  tags: [navigation, maps, fieldcraft]

- id: fm-5-125-rigging-techniques
  title: Rigging Techniques, Procedures and Applications
  publication_number: FM 5-125
  publisher: United States Army
  collections: [preparedness]
  distribution_status: verify-edition
  civilian_relevance: Rope, knots, slings, hoists, anchors, and mechanical advantage.
  tags: [knots, rigging, construction, fieldcraft]
source_documents.open-license.yaml:
- id: eff-surveillance-self-defense
  title: Surveillance Self-Defense
  publisher: Electronic Frontier Foundation
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: open-license
  claimed_license: CC BY
  distribution_status: verify-license-on-source
  description: Current guidance on threat modeling, private communications, metadata, and device security.
  snapshot_policy: Store review date, source URL, attribution, license text, and an unchanged snapshot where permitted.
  tags: [privacy, digital-security, encryption, censorship-circumvention]
source_documents.preparedness.yaml:

  source_id: ready-gov

  collections: [preparedness]

  catalog_status: candidate

  catalog_status: candidate

  why_included: Civilian navigation reference suitable for offline use.

source_documents.sources.yaml:
- id: project-gutenberg
  name: Project Gutenberg
  source_type: public-domain-text-repository
  base_url: https://www.gutenberg.org/
  use_for: Public-domain EPUB, HTML, and plain-text editions.
  acquisition_note: Record the exact ebook URL and retain no Project Gutenberg branding unless its terms permit it.

- id: standard-ebooks
  name: Standard Ebooks
  source_type: public-domain-ebook-repository
  base_url: https://standardebooks.org/
  use_for: Carefully prepared public-domain ebooks.
  acquisition_note: Verify the edition page and any project-specific redistribution terms.

- id: library-of-congress
  name: Library of Congress
  source_type: government-library
  base_url: https://www.loc.gov/
  use_for: Historical scans, U.S. Army field-manual inventory, and bibliographic verification.

- id: army-publishing-directorate
  name: Army Publishing Directorate
  source_type: government-publisher
  base_url: https://armypubs.army.mil/
  use_for: Current unrestricted Army publications.
  acquisition_note: Exclude restricted or authentication-only publications.

- id: ready-gov
  name: Ready.gov
  source_type: government-publisher
  base_url: https://www.ready.gov/
  use_for: FEMA preparedness guides and planning material.

- id: cdc
  name: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
  source_type: government-publisher
  base_url: https://www.cdc.gov/
  use_for: Current emergency water, sanitation, food-safety, and health guidance.

- id: usgs
  name: U.S. Geological Survey
  source_type: government-publisher
  base_url: https://www.usgs.gov/
  use_for: Map, compass, topographic-map, and geographic-reference material.

- id: eff
  name: Electronic Frontier Foundation
  source_type: rights-holder
  base_url: https://www.eff.org/
  use_for: Explicitly licensed digital-security material.
  acquisition_note: Confirm the license on the exact work or module before download.
source_documents.suppressed-knowledge.yaml:
- id: areopagitica
  title: Areopagitica
  author: John Milton
  original_year: 1644
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  description: A foundational argument against state licensing and prepublication censorship.
  why_included: Directly opposes systems that require official permission before publication.
  tags: [free-expression, censorship, political-philosophy, nonfiction]

- id: common-sense
  title: Common Sense
  author: Thomas Paine
  original_year: 1776
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  description: A direct argument that people may reject illegitimate political authority.
  why_included: Challenges hereditary rule and locates political authority in the people.
  tags: [revolution, political-philosophy, rights, nonfiction]

- id: rights-of-man
  title: Rights of Man
  author: Thomas Paine
  original_year: 1791
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  description: A defense of popular sovereignty and a critique of hereditary government.
  why_included: Provides a compact case for political rights independent of dynastic authority.
  tags: [rights, political-philosophy, revolution, nonfiction]

- id: civil-disobedience
  title: Civil Disobedience
  author: Henry David Thoreau
  original_year: 1849
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  description: An argument against cooperation with profoundly unjust government action.
  why_included: Defends individual conscience against coercive state power.
  tags: [resistance, rights, political-philosophy, nonfiction]

- id: two-treatises-of-government
  title: Two Treatises of Government
  author: John Locke
  original_year: 1689
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  description: A defense of natural rights, consent of the governed, and limits on political authority.
  why_included: Supplies an enduring argument for removing governments that violate public trust.
  tags: [rights, constitutionalism, political-philosophy, nonfiction]

- id: the-social-contract
  title: The Social Contract
  original_title: Du contrat social
  author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
  original_year: 1762
  original_language: French
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  translations:
    - language: English
      translator: G. D. H. Cole
      translation_year: 1913
      quality: recommended-pd-translation
      rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  description: An argument that legitimate political authority arises from collective popular sovereignty.
  why_included: Banned and burned for republicanism and its challenge to established political authority.
  tags: [rights, revolution, political-philosophy, nonfiction]

- id: the-republic
  title: The Republic
  original_title: Politeia
  author: Plato
  original_year: -380
  original_language: Ancient Greek
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge, original-language]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  translations:
    - language: English
      translator: Benjamin Jowett
      translation_year: 1871
      quality: serviceable-pd-translation
      rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  description: A dialogue about justice, government, education, censorship, art, and the ideal state.
  why_included: Often restricted in ideological settings; it is also a crucial source on the logic of censorship itself.
  tags: [political-philosophy, censorship, education, nonfiction]

- id: propaganda
  title: Propaganda
  author: Edward Bernays
  original_year: 1928
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  description: An early account of organized opinion management, symbols, authority, and mass persuasion.
  why_included: Helps readers identify techniques that governments and institutions use to shape public belief.
  tags: [propaganda, media-literacy, psychology, nonfiction]

- id: public-opinion
  title: Public Opinion
  author: Walter Lippmann
  original_year: 1922
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  description: An examination of stereotypes, media, expertise, and the construction of public belief.
  why_included: Gives readers tools for recognizing manufactured pictures of reality.
  tags: [propaganda, media-literacy, psychology, nonfiction]

- id: manual-for-the-solution-of-military-ciphers
  title: Manual for the Solution of Military Ciphers
  author: Parker Hitt
  original_year: 1916
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: pre-1931-work-candidate
  catalog_status: candidate
  description: A systematic historical guide to analyzing substitution and transposition ciphers.
  why_included: Preserves a practical foundation in private communication and cryptanalysis.
  tags: [cryptography, communications, mathematics, nonfiction]

- id: elementary-cryptanalysis
  title: Elementary Cryptanalysis
  author: Helen Fouché Gaines
  original_year: 1939
  original_language: English
  collections: [suppressed-knowledge]
  rights_status: copyrighted-link-only
  catalog_status: link-only
  description: A substantial reference on solving classical ciphers.
  why_included: Important historical cryptanalysis reference, but not yet U.S. public domain.
  tags: [cryptography, communications, mathematics, nonfiction]
